privacy-policy.title: Privacy Policy
paragraphs:
1. Privacy Policy
Effective Date: 1 July 2025 | Last Updated: 1 July 2025
1.1 Introduction
Charitey (referred to herein as 'we', 'us', 'our', or 'the Platform') is a digital social impact platform registered and operating under the laws of India. We are committed to protecting the privacy, confidentiality, and security of personal information entrusted to us by our users, including donors, non-governmental organisations (NGOs), volunteers, and website visitors.
This Privacy Policy explains in detail how Charitey collects, processes, stores, shares, and protects personal data when you use our website (charitey.org) and any future mobile applications or related digital services (collectively, 'the Platform'). By registering on or using the Platform, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Policy.
This policy is compliant with India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act) and draws from internationally recognised data protection principles. Where future regulations are enacted, this policy will be updated accordingly.
1.2 Definitions
For the purposes of this Privacy Policy, the following definitions apply:
'Personal Data' means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person, directly or indirectly, including name, contact details, identification numbers, or online identifiers.
'Processing' means any operation performed on personal data, including collection, storage, use, disclosure, or deletion.
'Data Principal' means the individual to whom personal data relates — the user of the Platform.
'Data Fiduciary' means Charitey, the entity that determines the purpose and means of processing personal data.
'Consent' means a freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous indication of agreement by a Data Principal.
'NGO' means a non-governmental organisation, non-profit, charitable trust, registered society, or similar entity.
'Donor' means an individual or organisation who donates goods, resources, or time through the Platform.
'Volunteer' means an individual who offers time, skills, or services through the Platform.
'Third Party' means any entity other than Charitey or the Data Principal.
'Platform' means the Charitey website and any future mobile applications or digital services.
1.3 Scope of This Policy
This Privacy Policy applies to:
All individuals who register an account on Charitey.
NGOs and charitable organisations that apply for verification on the Platform.
Individual and corporate donors who use the Platform to contribute resources.
Volunteers who register to offer time and services.
All visitors to the Charitey website, regardless of whether they register.
Any person who contacts Charitey via email, form submissions, or support channels.
This policy does not apply to third-party websites linked from the Platform. Charitey is not responsible for the privacy practices of external sites.
1.4 Information We Collect
1.4.1 Personal Information
When you create an account or engage with the Platform, we may collect the following personal information:
Full name and display name.
Email address and mobile number.
Date of birth (for age verification purposes where applicable).
Gender (optional, for personalisation).
Physical address or city and state of residence.
Profile photograph (optional).
Government-issued identification documents, where required for verification.
1.4.2 NGO Verification Information
NGOs applying for verified status on the Platform are required to submit:
Organisation name, registration number, and type (trust, society, Section 8 company, etc.).
Certificate of registration issued by the relevant government authority.
FCRA registration details, if applicable.
80G and 12A tax exemption certificates, if available.
Bank account details for the purpose of receiving in-kind logistics coordination (not financial transfers through the Platform).
Contact details of authorised representatives and trustees.
Organisation's operational scope, mission statement, and beneficiary profile.
Photographs and documentation of past activities.
1.4.3 Volunteer Information
Volunteers registering on the Platform may provide:
Name, contact details, and location.
Skills, qualifications, and areas of interest.
Availability and preferred engagement type.
Emergency contact information (optional).
Identity verification documents for certain roles.
1.4.4 Donor Information
Donors providing resources through the Platform may share:
Name and contact information.
Type, quantity, and condition of items being donated.
Preferred NGO or cause category.
Pickup address or drop-off preferences.
Corporate affiliation, if applicable.
1.5 Account Registration Data
During account registration, Charitey collects information necessary to create and maintain a secure account, including login credentials (stored in encrypted form), authentication details, account activity logs, and preferences. You may register as an individual donor, volunteer, or NGO representative. Each account type collects information appropriate to that role.
1.6 Technical and Device Information
When you visit the Platform, our servers and analytics systems may automatically collect technical data, including:
IP address and approximate geolocation derived from it.
Device type, operating system, and browser version.
Screen resolution and hardware identifiers.
Referral URL and exit URLs.
Pages visited, time spent, and interaction patterns.
Error logs and crash reports.
This information is used to maintain the security, performance, and functionality of the Platform and does not by itself identify you personally in most cases.
1.7 Cookies and Tracking Technologies
Charitey uses cookies and similar technologies (such as local storage and session identifiers) to enhance your experience. Please refer to our Cookie Policy (Section 4 of this document suite) for full details. In summary, we use:
Essential cookies required for the Platform to function.
Functional cookies that remember your preferences.
Analytics cookies to understand how users engage with the Platform.
Future marketing cookies, which will be introduced only with your explicit consent.
You may manage cookie preferences through your browser settings or via any consent mechanism we provide on the Platform.
1.8 Location Services
The Platform may request access to your approximate location in order to:
Match donors with geographically relevant NGOs.
Display volunteer opportunities near your area.
Facilitate logistics for resource pickup and delivery.
Location data is not shared with third parties without your consent and is processed only for purposes directly related to your use of the Platform. Location access via mobile applications (when introduced) will require explicit in-app permission.
1.9 Communications Data
When you contact Charitey through email, grievance forms, or other communication channels, we retain records of that correspondence, including the content of messages, your contact details, and timestamps, to enable us to respond to your enquiry and improve our service.
We may also send you transactional emails such as account creation confirmations, verification status updates, and activity notifications. Marketing communications will only be sent where you have provided explicit consent.
1.10 User-Generated Content
Content you submit to the Platform — including donation listings, resource requests, NGO profiles, photographs of goods, and comments — is stored by Charitey and may be displayed publicly on the Platform. You retain ownership of the content you create but grant Charitey a non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to display and use such content within the scope of Platform operations. You are responsible for ensuring that content you upload does not infringe third-party rights.
1.11 Information from Third Parties
We may receive information about you from third-party sources such as:
Social login providers, if you choose to sign in using a third-party account (e.g., Google).
Partner NGOs who may refer volunteers or donors to the Platform.
Government or public databases used to verify NGO registration details.
Analytics providers who may supplement our data with demographic insights.
Such third-party data is handled in accordance with this Privacy Policy and applicable data protection laws.
1.12 Legal Basis for Processing
Charitey processes personal data on the following legal bases:
Consent: Where you have given explicit consent for a specific processing purpose.
Contractual Necessity: Where processing is necessary to fulfil our obligations to you as a Platform user.
Legal Obligation: Where processing is required by law, such as responding to government or court orders.
Legitimate Interest: Where Charitey has a genuine and proportionate interest in processing your data, for example to prevent fraud, maintain platform security, and improve services — provided that our interests do not override your fundamental rights.
Public Interest: Where processing serves the broader social mission of facilitating charitable activities.
1.13 How We Use Your Information
Charitey uses the information it collects for the following purposes:
To create and manage your user account.
To verify the identity and legitimacy of NGOs registered on the Platform.
To facilitate connections between donors, NGOs, and volunteers.
To communicate platform updates, notifications, and relevant opportunities.
To process donation listings and resource requests.
To improve the usability, security, and features of the Platform.
To detect and prevent fraudulent, abusive, or illegal activity.
To comply with applicable laws and regulatory requirements.
To generate anonymised or aggregated statistical reports on Platform usage and social impact.
To respond to your support queries and grievances.
1.13.1 AI Processing
Charitey may in the future introduce AI-powered features, such as recommendation engines for matching donors with NGOs, automated moderation tools, or natural language processing for content review. Where such features are introduced, we will update this Privacy Policy to describe how AI systems process your data, your right to object to automated decision-making, and how to request human review of any automated decisions that affect you. No AI processing of personal data will be introduced without adequate notice and, where required, renewed consent.
1.14 Data Sharing
1.14.1 Third-Party Service Providers
Charitey may share personal data with trusted third-party service providers who assist us in operating the Platform. These may include:
Cloud infrastructure and hosting providers.
Email delivery and communication services.
Analytics and performance monitoring tools.
Fraud detection and identity verification services.
Customer support software providers.
All third-party processors are required to handle your data in accordance with applicable data protection laws and are bound by contractual obligations to maintain confidentiality and security.
1.14.2 Payment Providers
Charitey does not currently process financial transactions. The Platform facilitates the donation of physical goods and resources, not monetary transfers. Should financial donation features be introduced in the future, we will partner with licensed payment gateway providers and will update this Privacy Policy to reflect how payment-related personal data is processed, stored, and shared. Payment processing will comply with applicable RBI guidelines and PCI-DSS standards.
1.14.3 Government and Legal Requests
Charitey will disclose personal data to government authorities, courts, or law enforcement agencies where required by law, court order, or lawful authority. We will endeavour to notify affected users of such requests where legally permissible. We will not voluntarily share personal data with government agencies beyond what is legally required.
1.14.4 Business Transfers
In the event of a merger, acquisition, restructuring, or sale of assets, personal data held by Charitey may be transferred to the successor entity, subject to the same or equivalent privacy protections. Users will be notified of any such transfer prior to its completion.
1.15 Security Measures
1.15.1 Encryption
All data transmitted between your browser or device and our servers is encrypted using industry-standard Transport Layer Security (TLS). Sensitive data, including passwords and identity documents, is encrypted at rest using strong encryption algorithms. We do not store passwords in plaintext.
1.15.2 Authentication
Charitey employs secure authentication practices, including hashed password storage, optional multi-factor authentication, and session management controls. Accounts are locked after repeated failed login attempts to prevent unauthorised access.
1.15.3 Access Controls
Access to personal data within Charitey is restricted on a need-to-know basis. Internal team members are granted access only to the data necessary to perform their designated functions. We conduct periodic reviews of access permissions.
1.16 Data Retention
Charitey retains personal data for as long as is necessary to fulfil the purpose for which it was collected or as required by law. Specifically:
Active account data is retained for the duration of your account registration.
NGO verification documents are retained for the period of verification and for a reasonable period thereafter.
Communications data is retained for up to three years from the date of correspondence.
Analytics and technical data may be retained in anonymised form indefinitely.
Data associated with closed accounts is removed within 90 days, subject to legal retention requirements.
You may request deletion of your account and associated personal data at any time. Please refer to Section 1.17 for user rights.
1.17 User Rights
Under applicable Indian data protection law and our own commitment to user privacy, you have the following rights:
Right to Access: You may request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
Right to Correction: You may request correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal data.
Right to Erasure: You may request deletion of your personal data, subject to legal retention requirements.
Right to Withdraw Consent: Where processing is based on consent, you may withdraw that consent at any time.
Right to Data Portability: You may request your personal data in a commonly used, machine-readable format.
Right to Grievance Redressal: You may file a complaint with our designated Grievance Officer.
1.17.1 Data Access Requests
To exercise any of the above rights, please submit a written request to our Grievance Officer at privacy@charitey.org. We will respond to all verified requests within 30 days. We may require you to verify your identity before processing your request.
1.17.2 Account Deletion
You may delete your account at any time via your account settings or by submitting a request to support@charitey.org. Account deletion will result in the removal of your profile, personal information, and user-generated content, except where retention is required by law or for the resolution of ongoing disputes. See our Data Deletion Policy in Section 6 for full details.
1.18 Children's Privacy
The Charitey Platform is not intended for use by individuals under the age of 18 years. We do not knowingly collect personal data from minors. If we become aware that personal data has been collected from a person under 18 without appropriate consent, we will promptly delete such data. Parents or guardians who believe their child's data has been collected without consent should contact us immediately at privacy@charitey.org.
1.19 International Data Transfers
Charitey's primary data processing operations are conducted within India. However, certain third-party service providers (such as cloud hosting and analytics services) may process data in other jurisdictions. Where data is transferred internationally, we ensure adequate safeguards are in place through contractual data protection clauses and ensuring that recipients adhere to data protection standards comparable to those required under Indian law.
1.20 Marketing Communications
Charitey will not send marketing communications without your explicit prior consent. Where you have opted in to receive updates, newsletters, or platform announcements, you may withdraw that consent at any time by:
Using the unsubscribe link included in every marketing email.
Updating your communication preferences in your account settings.
Contacting us directly at support@charitey.org.
1.21 Push Notifications
The Charitey mobile application (when launched) may request permission to send push notifications regarding donation matches, volunteer opportunities, and platform updates. You may grant or revoke push notification permissions at any time through your device settings. Push notifications will not be used for marketing purposes without explicit consent.
1.22 Changes to This Policy
Charitey reserves the right to update or amend this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our data practices, services, or applicable law. We will notify you of material changes by posting a notice on the Platform and, where appropriate, by email. Continued use of the Platform following the publication of changes constitutes acceptance of the revised policy. We encourage you to review this policy periodically.
1.23 Governing Law
This Privacy Policy is governed by the laws of India, including the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, the Information Technology Act, 2000 and its amendments, and any regulations issued thereunder. Disputes arising from this policy shall be subject to the jurisdiction of competent courts located in [City, State], India.
1.24 Contact Details
Grievance Officer: grievance@charitey.org
Privacy Enquiries: privacy@charitey.org
General Support: support@charitey.org
Postal Address: [Registered Address, City, State, PIN Code], India
1.25 Consent
By using the Charitey Platform, creating an account, or submitting information through our forms, you confirm that you have read and understood this Privacy Policy and that you consent to the collection, processing, and use of your personal data as described herein. Where applicable, we will seek your specific consent for processing activities that require it, and you retain the right to withdraw such consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out prior to withdrawal.

terms-and-conditions.title: Terms & Conditions
paragraphs:
1. Terms and Conditions
Effective Date: 1 July 2025 | Last Updated: 1 July 2025
2.1 Acceptance of Terms
These Terms and Conditions ('Terms') govern your access to and use of the Charitey Platform operated by Charitey ('we', 'us', 'our'). By accessing, registering on, or using the Platform in any capacity — whether as a visitor, donor, volunteer, or NGO — you agree to be bound by these Terms in their entirety. If you do not agree to these Terms, you must immediately cease using the Platform.
These Terms constitute a legally binding agreement between you and Charitey. We recommend that you read them carefully and retain a copy for your records. These Terms should be read in conjunction with our Privacy Policy, Community Guidelines, and all other policies published on the Platform.
2.2 Definitions
In these Terms and Conditions:
'Platform' means the Charitey website at charitey.org and any future mobile applications or digital services.
'User' means any individual or entity accessing or using the Platform.
'Account' means a registered user profile created on the Platform.
'NGO' means a non-governmental organisation, charitable trust, registered society, or non-profit company verified on the Platform.
'Donor' means an individual or corporate entity contributing goods or resources through the Platform.
'Volunteer' means an individual registering to offer time or services through the Platform.
'Content' means all text, images, data, listings, and other material submitted to or displayed on the Platform.
'Donation Listing' means a post by a donor offering specific goods or resources for charitable distribution.
'Resource Request' means a post by an NGO describing a need for specific goods, services, or volunteers.
'Charitey' means the team, organisation, and legal entity operating the Platform.
2.3 Eligibility
To use the Charitey Platform you must:
Be at least 18 years of age.
Be legally capable of entering into a binding agreement.
Not be prohibited by any applicable law from using the Platform.
Provide accurate, truthful, and complete information during registration.
For NGOs: be a validly registered non-profit entity under applicable Indian law.
Charitey reserves the right to refuse registration to any individual or organisation at its sole discretion, including where eligibility requirements are not met.
2.4 User Accounts
2.4.1 Registration
To access most features of the Platform, you must create an account. During registration, you agree to provide accurate and current information and to keep such information updated. You are solely responsible for all activity that occurs under your account. You must not share your login credentials with any other person or permit any unauthorised access to your account.
Charitey is not liable for any loss or damage arising from your failure to protect your account credentials. If you suspect unauthorised access to your account, you must notify us immediately at security@charitey.org.
2.4.2 Account Verification
Certain account types — particularly NGO accounts — are subject to a verification process. NGOs must submit the documentation described in our NGO Verification Guidelines. Verification does not constitute an endorsement by Charitey of the NGO's activities or financial practices. It indicates only that the submitted documentation has been reviewed in good faith. Charitey reserves the right to revoke verified status at any time if documentation is found to be fraudulent or if the NGO violates these Terms.
2.5 NGO Responsibilities
NGOs registered on the Platform agree to:
Maintain the accuracy of all information provided in their profile and verification submission.
Respond promptly to donation offers and volunteer enquiries.
Use donated goods and resources solely for the stated charitable purpose.
Not misrepresent the nature, urgency, or recipients of any resource request.
Notify Charitey immediately of any change in registration status, legal standing, or key personnel.
Comply with all applicable Indian laws governing non-profit organisations.
Not use the Platform to collect or redirect financial donations unless Charitey has specifically enabled such functionality.
Provide Charitey with reasonable evidence of goods received and their intended use upon request.
2.6 Donor Responsibilities
Donors using the Platform agree to:
Donate only goods that are in a safe, usable, and legal condition.
Accurately describe the type, quantity, and condition of donated goods.
Not attempt to donate prohibited, hazardous, expired, counterfeit, or illegal items.
Complete donation arrangements in a timely manner once a connection has been made.
Not list the same donation simultaneously on multiple platforms if it creates a risk of double-claiming.
Treat NGO representatives and volunteers with respect.
Ensure that goods donated to vulnerable populations (food, medicines, clothing) meet appropriate safety standards.
2.7 Volunteer Responsibilities
Volunteers using the Platform agree to:
Provide accurate information about their skills, availability, and qualifications.
Honour commitments made to NGOs in a timely and professional manner.
Conduct themselves with honesty, respect, and professionalism at all times.
Not misrepresent their qualifications, particularly for roles requiring specific expertise.
Report any concerns regarding the conduct of NGOs or other Platform users to Charitey.
Comply with any applicable health, safety, or legal requirements when performing volunteer activities.
2.8 Acceptable Use
By using the Platform, you agree that you will:
Use the Platform only for lawful charitable and social impact purposes.
Not upload or transmit any content that is false, defamatory, misleading, or fraudulent.
Respect the rights, privacy, and dignity of all other users.
Comply with all applicable laws and regulations.
Not attempt to circumvent any security or access control measures.
Not use the Platform in a manner that could damage or impair its availability or performance.
2.9 Prohibited Activities
The following activities are strictly prohibited on the Charitey Platform:
Creating false, duplicate, or misleading accounts.
Misrepresenting your identity, affiliation, or credentials.
Submitting fraudulent NGO verification documents.
Using the Platform to solicit financial donations outside of authorised channels.
Collecting, harvesting, or scraping user data without authorisation.
Sending unsolicited communications or spam to other users.
Uploading malicious code, viruses, or harmful content.
Engaging in or facilitating illegal activities of any kind.
Discriminating against users on the basis of religion, caste, gender, disability, or any protected characteristic.
Posting hate speech, obscene content, or material that incites violence.
Attempting to defraud donors, NGOs, or volunteers.
Misusing the reporting system to target legitimate users.
Using the Platform for personal financial gain outside its intended social impact purpose.
2.10 Intellectual Property
2.10.1 Platform Ownership
All intellectual property rights in the Charitey Platform — including but not limited to the name, logo, design, layout, software, code, database structure, and original content — are owned by Charitey or its licensors. Nothing in these Terms constitutes a transfer of any intellectual property rights to you.
You are granted a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable licence to access and use the Platform solely for its intended purpose, subject to these Terms. This licence does not permit you to reproduce, modify, distribute, or create derivative works of any Platform content without prior written consent.
2.10.2 User Content Licence
By submitting content to the Platform, you grant Charitey a non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide licence to host, display, and reproduce such content for the purpose of operating and promoting the Platform. You represent and warrant that you have the right to grant this licence and that your content does not infringe the intellectual property or privacy rights of any third party.
2.10.3 Trademarks
'Charitey' and associated logos and marks are trademarks or registered trademarks of the Platform operator. You may not use these marks in any way that implies endorsement, affiliation, or sponsorship by Charitey without prior written consent.
2.11 Donations and Resource Requests
Charitey acts as a facilitator connecting donors with NGOs. We do not own, take possession of, or guarantee the quality of donated goods. We do not verify the condition of items listed by donors and cannot guarantee that any donation will be accepted or that any resource request will be fulfilled.
Donors enter into their own arrangements with NGOs regarding the logistics of donation. Charitey bears no liability for loss, damage, or non-delivery of goods. In the event of a dispute between a donor and an NGO, Charitey may attempt to facilitate resolution but is not obligated to do so and is not a party to the transaction.
2.12 Platform Availability
Charitey endeavours to maintain the availability and performance of the Platform at all times. However, we do not guarantee uninterrupted access. The Platform may be temporarily unavailable due to:
Scheduled maintenance and upgrades.
Unscheduled technical failures or outages.
Third-party service provider disruptions.
Force majeure events beyond our control.
Charitey will use reasonable efforts to minimise disruption and to notify users of planned outages in advance. We are not liable for any loss or inconvenience resulting from Platform unavailability.
2.13 Limitation of Liability
To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law:
Charitey's total liability to you for any claim arising from or related to your use of the Platform shall not exceed INR 1,000 (Indian Rupees One Thousand).
Charitey shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, consequential, punitive, or special damages, including loss of data, revenue, or goodwill.
Charitey makes no warranties, express or implied, regarding the Platform's fitness for a particular purpose, merchantability, or accuracy of content.
Charitey is not responsible for the actions, omissions, conduct, or content of any NGO, donor, or volunteer.
Nothing in these Terms limits liability that cannot be excluded by law, including liability for fraud or death or personal injury caused by negligence.
2.14 Indemnification
You agree to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Charitey and its directors, employees, agents, and partners from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of or related to:
Your breach of these Terms.
Your violation of any applicable law or regulation.
Content you submit to the Platform.
Your interaction with another user or NGO.
Any misrepresentation of your identity or credentials.
2.15 Suspension and Termination
Charitey reserves the right to suspend or permanently terminate your account, with or without prior notice, if:
You breach these Terms or any Platform policy.
We reasonably suspect fraudulent or illegal activity.
You engage in behaviour that is harmful to other users or the Platform.
Your continued use poses a risk to the security or integrity of the Platform.
Upon termination, your right to access the Platform ceases immediately. Termination does not relieve you of obligations incurred prior to the date of termination, including indemnification obligations. You may appeal termination decisions by contacting grievance@charitey.org.

community-guidelines.title: Community Guidelines
paragraphs:
3. Community Guidelines
Effective Date: 1 July 2025 | Last Updated: 1 July 2025
3.1 Our Community Vision
Charitey exists to bring together individuals, organisations, and communities with a shared purpose: to ensure that essential resources reach those who need them most. Our Platform is built on the principles of trust, generosity, dignity, and collaboration. These Community Guidelines define the standards of behaviour that every participant — whether an NGO, donor, volunteer, or visitor — is expected to uphold.
These Guidelines are not exhaustive. They reflect the spirit in which the Platform was built. We ask all users to engage with honesty, compassion, and responsibility, even in situations not explicitly addressed here.
3.2 Respectful Conduct
Every person who uses the Charitey Platform deserves to be treated with dignity and respect. We expect all users to:
Communicate courteously and professionally with NGOs, donors, volunteers, and Platform staff.
Acknowledge that beneficiaries of charitable resources are members of our communities deserving of full respect and dignity.
Avoid language, tone, or imagery that is condescending, stereotyping, or reduces individuals to their circumstances.
Resolve misunderstandings through calm and constructive dialogue.
Respect cultural, linguistic, and regional differences among Platform users across India.
Charitey serves users across diverse communities throughout India. We expect sensitivity to socioeconomic, cultural, and regional differences, and we will not tolerate any conduct that diminishes the worth or dignity of any individual or group.
3.3 Ethical Donations
Donors play a vital role in the Charitey ecosystem. We ask all donors to:
Donate only items that are genuinely useful, safe, and in reasonable condition.
Ensure that food items are within their use-by date and have been stored hygienically.
Verify that clothing items are clean, intact, and appropriate for the intended recipients.
Confirm that medical supplies are unexpired, properly stored, and intended for general use.
Not donate items that are broken, hazardous, expired, or that you would not personally use in the same circumstances.
Be transparent about the condition and provenance of donated goods.
Avoid donating items with the expectation of publicity, recognition, or personal benefit.
Ethical donation means considering the needs and circumstances of recipients rather than simply discarding unwanted possessions. Please donate with thought and care.
3.4 Honest Resource Requests
NGOs and individuals submitting resource requests are required to:
Accurately describe the nature, quantity, and urgency of the items or support needed.
Submit requests only for genuine needs of verified beneficiary populations.
Not exaggerate or fabricate urgency to attract more donations.
Update or close requests when needs have been fulfilled.
Not submit duplicate or overlapping requests for the same need.
Provide Charitey with reasonable evidence of receipt and use of donated resources upon request.
3.5 No Fraud
Fraudulent activity of any kind is strictly prohibited and will result in immediate account suspension and referral to appropriate law enforcement authorities. Fraud includes, but is not limited to:
Creating fake NGO profiles or submitting forged registration documents.
Misappropriating donated goods for personal gain.
Submitting false resource requests to accumulate goods without a genuine charitable purpose.
Impersonating a legitimate organisation or individual.
Manipulating Platform features to gain unfair advantages.
Soliciting cash donations under the guise of goods-based requests.
If you suspect fraud on the Platform, please report it immediately to fraud@charitey.org. Charitey takes all fraud reports seriously and will investigate promptly.
3.6 No Hate Speech
The Charitey Platform is a space of compassion and solidarity. We have zero tolerance for:
Hate speech targeting individuals or groups based on religion, caste, gender, sexual orientation, race, nationality, disability, or any other protected characteristic.
Content that dehumanises, demeans, or incites hostility toward any group.
Stereotyping or discriminatory language in donation listings, resource requests, NGO profiles, or communications.
Symbols, imagery, or references associated with hate groups or ideologies.
Content that violates our hate speech policy will be removed immediately, and repeat offenders will be permanently banned from the Platform.
3.7 No Harassment
Charitey is committed to providing a safe environment for all users. Harassment of any kind is prohibited, including:
Sending unsolicited or unwelcome messages to other users.
Targeting a user with repeated negative interactions or reports.
Making threats, whether explicit or implied.
Sharing personal information about another user without their consent ('doxxing').
Sexually inappropriate communications or behaviour.
Intimidation of volunteers, NGO staff, or beneficiaries.
If you experience harassment on the Platform, please use our reporting mechanism or contact support@charitey.org. We will treat all harassment complaints confidentially and act promptly.
3.8 No Illegal Activities
The Platform must not be used to facilitate any activity that is illegal under Indian law or the laws of any jurisdiction. Prohibited activities include:
Trafficking in stolen, counterfeit, or prohibited goods.
Distributing controlled substances or items requiring regulatory approval.
Money laundering or financial fraud.
Circumventing FCRA regulations governing foreign contributions to NGOs.
Violating intellectual property laws.
Any form of exploitation, including child labour or forced labour.
3.9 Authentic Information
The trust that makes Charitey effective depends entirely on the authenticity of the information shared on the Platform. All users must:
Provide truthful and accurate information in profiles, listings, and communications.
Not impersonate another person, NGO, or government entity.
Not create multiple accounts to circumvent restrictions or bans.
Update their information promptly when circumstances change.
Not use artificial means (bots, scripts, purchased reviews) to manipulate Platform metrics or visibility.
3.10 Safe Communication
For your protection and the protection of others:
Do not share sensitive personal information (such as financial account details, Aadhaar numbers, or passwords) through Platform messaging.
Be cautious of any request that seems unusual or that asks you to communicate or transact outside the Platform.
Report suspicious communications to Charitey before taking any action.
Charitey staff will never ask for your password or payment details.
3.11 Volunteer Behaviour
Volunteers are ambassadors for the spirit of Charitey. When undertaking voluntary activities facilitated through the Platform, volunteers must:
Arrive prepared, on time, and ready to fulfil their commitment.
Behave professionally and respectfully in all interactions with NGO staff, beneficiaries, and partner organisations.
Not photograph or record beneficiaries without explicit, informed consent.
Respect the confidentiality of information encountered during volunteer activities.
Report any concerns about NGO practices, safety conditions, or ethical issues to Charitey.
Not engage in any activity that exploits or harms the communities they are serving.
3.12 NGO Standards
Verified NGOs on the Platform are held to a higher standard of accountability, given the trust placed in them by donors and volunteers. NGOs must:
Operate transparently and share information about their activities openly.
Utilise donated goods solely for the charitable purpose stated in their profile and resource requests.
Maintain proper records of donations received and their distribution.
Treat donors, volunteers, and beneficiaries with respect and professionalism.
Not use donated goods for commercial resale unless explicitly disclosed and agreed by donors.
Respond promptly to enquiries from Charitey's compliance team.
Immediately notify Charitey of any change in legal status, leadership, or operational scope.
3.13 Reporting Violations
If you observe conduct that violates these Community Guidelines, please report it through one of the following channels:
Use the 'Report' function available on all listings, profiles, and messages.
Email our compliance team at report@charitey.org.
Contact our Grievance Officer at grievance@charitey.org for serious concerns.
All reports are reviewed by our moderation team. We do not tolerate the abuse of the reporting system to target legitimate users, and such misuse will itself be treated as a violation of these Guidelines.
3.14 Enforcement Actions
Violations of these Community Guidelines may result in the following actions, applied at Charitey's discretion and proportional to the severity of the breach:
Warning: A formal notice advising the user of the violation and expected correction.
Content Removal: Removal of listings, requests, or other content that violates Guidelines.
Temporary Suspension: Temporary restriction of account access pending investigation.

cookie-policy.title: Cookie Policy
paragraphs:
4. Cookie Policy
Effective Date: 1 July 2025 | Last Updated: 1 July 2025
4.1 What Are Cookies?
Cookies are small text files placed on your device (computer, tablet, or smartphone) by websites you visit. They are widely used to make websites function correctly, to remember your preferences, and to provide information to site owners about how the site is being used. Cookies cannot execute programs or deliver viruses to your device. They are uniquely assigned to you and can only be read by the web server that issued them.
In addition to cookies, Charitey may use similar technologies including local storage, session storage, and web beacons, all of which function in broadly similar ways. References to 'cookies' in this policy encompass these technologies unless otherwise specified.
4.2 Essential Cookies
Essential cookies are strictly necessary for the Charitey Platform to function. Without them, core services such as logging in, navigating between pages, and submitting forms would not work. These cookies do not collect personal information and cannot be switched off without impairing Platform functionality. Examples include:
Session cookies that maintain your login state while you browse.
CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery) tokens that protect your account from malicious requests.
Load balancing cookies that direct requests to the appropriate server.
Security cookies that flag suspicious activity.
No consent is required for essential cookies under applicable law, as they are strictly necessary for the service you have requested.
4.3 Functional Cookies
Functional cookies allow the Platform to remember choices you have made and provide enhanced, personalised features. They may be set by Charitey or by third-party providers whose services we have integrated. Examples include:
Language and regional preference settings.
Remembering your login details (if you select 'stay logged in').
Storing display preferences such as accessibility settings.
These cookies are generally set only with your consent, except where they are necessary to provide a feature you have specifically requested.
4.4 Analytics Cookies
Analytics cookies help us understand how visitors interact with the Platform by collecting and reporting information anonymously. We use this data to improve the Platform's design, content, and performance. Information collected may include:
Which pages are visited most frequently.
How long users spend on each page.
Where users come from (referral source).
Which device and browser users are using.
Whether users complete key actions (such as submitting a donation listing).
Analytics data is aggregated and does not personally identify individual users. We may use tools such as Google Analytics or equivalent privacy-compliant analytics platforms. Where such tools are used, they are configured to anonymise IP addresses and comply with applicable data protection law.
4.5 Marketing Cookies
Charitey does not currently use marketing or advertising cookies. In the future, if we introduce paid advertising, partnerships, or targeted outreach campaigns, we may deploy marketing cookies to measure the effectiveness of campaigns and to show relevant content. Any introduction of marketing cookies will:
Be preceded by an update to this Cookie Policy and appropriate notice to users.
Require your explicit prior consent through a clear consent mechanism.
Be subject to your right to withdraw consent at any time.
4.6 Cookie Consent
When you first visit the Platform, you will be presented with a cookie consent notice. By clicking 'Accept All', you consent to the use of all cookie categories described in this policy. You may also customise your preferences by selecting specific categories. Choosing 'Reject Non-Essential' will limit cookies to essential cookies only.
Your consent is stored and respected on subsequent visits. You may change your cookie preferences at any time by accessing the cookie settings panel available in the website footer.
4.7 Managing Cookies Through Browser Settings
Most web browsers allow you to manage cookie preferences through their settings. You can typically:
View all cookies stored by websites.
Delete individual or all cookies.
Block all cookies from specific websites.
Set preferences to be notified before a cookie is placed.
Please note that blocking essential cookies will impair your ability to use the Platform. Instructions for managing cookies in popular browsers can be found through the browser's help documentation. Clearing cookies will also remove any saved preferences or login sessions.
4.8 Third-Party Cookies
Some cookies on the Platform are set by third-party service providers, including analytics providers and social sharing tools. These third parties have their own privacy and cookie policies. Charitey does not control the cookies set by third parties and recommends that you review the relevant third-party policies. Currently, third-party cookies may be set by analytics services. We will maintain an updated list of third-party cookie providers in our Platform's cookie management interface.
4.9 Changes to This Cookie Policy
We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time to reflect changes in technology, regulation, or our Platform's features. Material changes will be communicated via the Platform and, where required, renewed consent will be sought. The 'Last Updated' date at the top of this document will reflect any changes.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
paragraphs:
5. Disclaimer
Effective Date: 1 July 2025 | Last Updated: 1 July 2025
5.1 General Information
The information provided on the Charitey Platform is intended solely for the purpose of facilitating charitable connections between donors, NGOs, and volunteers. Charitey makes reasonable efforts to ensure that the content published on the Platform is accurate, current, and complete. However, information is provided on an 'as is' basis without any warranties of any kind, express or implied.
Nothing on the Platform constitutes legal, financial, medical, or professional advice. Users should seek appropriate professional guidance before making decisions based on information found on the Platform.
5.2 Platform Limitations
Charitey is a facilitating platform and not a party to any donation, resource transfer, or volunteering arrangement made between users. We do not:
Own, possess, inspect, or guarantee any goods listed for donation.
Guarantee the accuracy of information provided by NGOs, donors, or volunteers.
Guarantee that any resource request will be fulfilled or any donation will be accepted.
Verify the current operational status of NGOs beyond the documentation submitted for initial verification.
Mediate or arbitrate disputes between users, though we may offer assistance.
5.3 No Guarantees
Charitey does not guarantee:
That the Platform will be available at all times or free from errors.
That information provided by users is accurate, current, or complete.
That donated goods will be of any particular quality or condition.
That NGOs will utilise donations as described in their profiles.
That volunteer engagements will proceed as arranged.
Any specific outcome or social impact from any transaction facilitated through the Platform.
5.4 Third-Party Content
The Platform may display content provided by users, NGOs, or third-party organisations. Charitey does not endorse, verify, or take responsibility for third-party content. Any opinions, recommendations, or representations made by users or NGOs are their own and do not represent the views of Charitey. Links to external websites are provided for convenience only and do not constitute endorsement.
5.5 Website Availability and Technical Errors
Charitey takes reasonable steps to maintain the availability and security of the Platform. However, we cannot guarantee uninterrupted service and accept no liability for:
Downtime, interruptions, or unavailability of the Platform.
Loss of data arising from technical failures.
Errors or inaccuracies introduced by technical processing.
Delays in processing submissions or communications.
5.6 External Links
The Platform may contain links to websites operated by third parties. These links are provided for your information and convenience only. Charitey has no control over the content of third-party websites and accepts no responsibility for any loss, damage, or inconvenience arising from your use of external websites. The inclusion of any link does not imply endorsement of the linked website or its content.
5.7 Future Feature Disclaimer
Charitey is an evolving platform. Features described as 'coming soon', 'planned', or 'future' are aspirational and subject to change without notice. References to planned features — including mobile applications, AI-powered matching, payment processing, or advanced analytics — do not constitute a commitment to deliver those features within any specified timeframe. Charitey accepts no liability arising from reliance on any statement regarding future functionality.

accessibility.title: Accessibility Statement
paragraphs:
8. Accessibility Statement
Effective Date: 1 July 2025 | Last Updated: 1 July 2025
8.1 Accessibility Commitment
Charitey is committed to making its Platform accessible to all users, regardless of disability, age, assistive technology use, or level of digital literacy. We believe that social impact tools should be available to everyone, and we are dedicated to removing barriers that prevent people from participating in the Platform's charitable mission.
8.2 Standards
We aim to conform to the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 at Level AA as published by the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C). These guidelines describe four key principles of accessible web design:
Perceivable: Information and user interface components must be presentable to users in ways they can perceive.
Operable: User interface components and navigation must be operable.
Understandable: Information and the operation of the user interface must be understandable.
Robust: Content must be robust enough to be interpreted by a wide variety of user agents, including assistive technologies.
8.3 Specific Accessibility Features
Charitey endeavours to implement the following accessibility features on the Platform:
Screen Reader Support: All key content, forms, buttons, and interactive elements are labelled to support screen reader software.
Keyboard Navigation: The Platform can be navigated using a keyboard alone, without requiring the use of a mouse.
Text Alternatives: Images, icons, and non-text elements include descriptive alternative text.
Colour Contrast: Text and background colours are selected to meet minimum contrast ratios.
Resizable Text: Text can be resized up to 200% without loss of content or functionality.
Form Accessibility: All forms include clear labels, instructions, and error messages.
Consistent Navigation: Navigation menus and structures are consistent across the Platform.
8.4 Limitations
While we strive to meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA, Charitey is a new and evolving Platform. There may be areas where our current implementation falls short of these standards. We are actively working to identify and address any accessibility gaps and will prioritise improvements as part of our development roadmap.
8.5 Ongoing Improvements
Charitey's accessibility programme includes:
Regular accessibility audits of the Platform by our development team.
User testing with individuals who use assistive technologies.
Staff training on accessible design and content creation.
Scheduled reviews of this Accessibility Statement to reflect current Platform status.
Inclusion of accessibility considerations in the design and development of future features, including the planned mobile application.
8.6 Feedback
We welcome feedback from users about their accessibility experience on the Platform. If you encounter accessibility barriers or have suggestions for improvement, please contact us at:
Email: accessibility@charitey.org
Subject: Accessibility Feedback
We aim to acknowledge accessibility feedback within 5 business days and to provide a substantive response within 21 business days.

data-deletion-policy.title: Data Deletion Policy
paragraphs:
6. Data Deletion Policy
Effective Date: 1 July 2025 | Last Updated: 1 July 2025
6.1 Account Deletion
Users may request deletion of their Charitey account at any time. Account deletion requests can be submitted via:
The account settings panel, using the 'Delete Account' option.
A written request to support@charitey.org with the subject line 'Account Deletion Request'.
A written request to our Grievance Officer at grievance@charitey.org.
Upon receiving a deletion request, Charitey will verify the identity of the requester before initiating the deletion process. We will acknowledge your request within 5 business days.
6.2 What Is Deleted
Upon account deletion, the following categories of data will be removed from active systems:
Your name, email address, contact details, and profile information.
Donation listings and resource requests you have submitted.
Profile photographs and uploaded documents (except those required to be retained by law).
Account activity logs, preferences, and saved settings.
Direct messages and Platform communications associated with your account.
6.3 Retention Period Before Full Deletion
Following submission of a deletion request, Charitey will initiate deletion within 30 days. In some cases, where we must verify outstanding obligations or investigate complaints, this period may be extended to a maximum of 90 days. During this period, your account will be deactivated and inaccessible to other users.
6.4 Backup Removal
Data deleted from active systems may persist in encrypted backup copies for a period of up to 90 additional days following active deletion, after which it will be permanently removed from all backup storage. During this backup retention period, the data will not be used for any active processing.
6.5 Legal Exceptions to Deletion
Charitey may be required to retain certain data beyond the standard deletion period in order to:
Comply with legal obligations under applicable Indian law.
Respond to ongoing government or regulatory investigations.
Resolve pending legal disputes or claims.
Maintain records required for tax or audit purposes.
Prevent fraud or abuse by individuals who have been permanently banned.
Where data is retained for legal purposes, it will be held securely and will not be used for any other purpose. Users will be informed of the reason for extended retention where permitted by law.
6.6 NGO Data
NGO verification documents and associated records may be retained for a period of up to 5 years following account closure, in accordance with applicable regulatory requirements and to protect the integrity of the Platform's verification history.
6.7 Processing Timeline Summary
Day 1: Deletion request received and acknowledged.
Days 1–5: Identity verification completed.
Days 5–30: Active data deletion initiated and completed.
Days 30–120: Backup data purged from encrypted backups.
Day 120+: All personal data permanently removed (unless legal exception applies).
6.8 Contact for Data Deletion
For all data deletion requests and enquiries, contact:
Email: charitey12@gmail.com
Subject: Data Deletion Request

acceptable-use-policy.title: Acceptable Use Policy
paragraphs:
7. Acceptable Use Policy
Effective Date: 1 July 2025 | Last Updated: 1 July 2025
7.1 Purpose
This Acceptable Use Policy ('AUP') describes the standards of conduct that all Charitey Platform users must adhere to. It supplements our Terms and Conditions and Community Guidelines. By using the Platform, you agree to comply with this AUP.
7.2 Proper Platform Usage
The Platform is designed exclusively for the purpose of facilitating charitable connections between donors, NGOs, and volunteers in India. You must use the Platform only for this intended purpose. All interactions, submissions, and communications on the Platform should be genuine, lawful, and consistent with the social impact mission of Charitey.
7.3 User Responsibilities
Each user is individually responsible for:
Ensuring that all information they provide is accurate and up to date.
Maintaining the security of their account credentials.
Ensuring that their use of the Platform complies with all applicable laws.
Promptly reporting any security vulnerabilities or suspicious activity to security@charitey.org.
7.4 Security
Users must not:
Attempt to gain unauthorised access to any part of the Platform or to any other user's account.
Probe, scan, or test the security of the Platform without express written authorisation.
Introduce malware, trojans, ransomware, or other harmful code.
Interfere with or disrupt the integrity or performance of the Platform or its servers.
Use the Platform in a manner that could expose it to distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks or other forms of cyberattack.
Bypass or circumvent any authentication, encryption, or access control mechanism.
7.5 Spam and Unsolicited Communications
Users must not:
Send unsolicited bulk messages to other users.
Use Platform messaging features for commercial solicitation unrelated to charitable activities.
Submit repetitive or duplicate listings designed to inflate visibility.
Use the Platform to recruit users to third-party commercial platforms.
7.6 Fraud and Misrepresentation
Users must not engage in any fraudulent activity, including:
Creating accounts under false identities.
Misrepresenting the nature, condition, or availability of donated goods.
Fabricating NGO activities or beneficiary populations.
Submitting false documents for verification purposes.
Redirecting charitable resources for personal gain.
7.7 Automated Access and Scraping
The following are strictly prohibited without prior written consent from Charitey:
The use of bots, crawlers, scrapers, or automated scripts to access, collect, or index Platform content.
Automated posting, messaging, or interaction with the Platform.
Data harvesting for the purpose of creating competing platforms or databases.
Using the Platform's API (when available) beyond the scope of permitted use.
7.8 Illegal Activities
The Platform must not be used to facilitate any illegal activity, including but not limited to:
Money laundering, tax evasion, or financial fraud.
Trade in prohibited or restricted goods.
Violation of foreign contribution regulations (FCRA).
Any activity constituting an offence under the Indian Penal Code, Information Technology Act, or any other applicable statute.
7.9 Enforcement
Violations of this AUP will result in appropriate enforcement action, including account suspension, permanent banning, and referral to law enforcement authorities. Charitey reserves the right to monitor Platform activity to detect and prevent AUP violations, subject to applicable privacy laws.

copyright-policy.title: Copyright Policy
paragraphs:
9. Copyright Policy
Effective Date: 1 July 2025 | Last Updated: 1 July 2025
9.1 Ownership of Platform Content
All intellectual property rights in the Charitey Platform — including the software, design, layout, original written content, logos, graphics, and trademarks — are owned by Charitey or its licensors and are protected under the Copyright Act, 1957 (India) and other applicable intellectual property laws. Unauthorised reproduction, distribution, or modification of Charitey's intellectual property is strictly prohibited.
9.2 User-Generated Content
Users who submit content to the Platform — including photographs of donated goods, NGO activity reports, and descriptions — retain ownership of their original content. By submitting content to Charitey, you grant us a non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to host, display, and reproduce the content in connection with Platform operations. You represent and warrant that:
You are the original creator of the content or have all necessary rights to submit it.
The content does not infringe the copyright, trademark, privacy, or other rights of any third party.
You are authorised to grant the licence described above.
9.3 Copyright Complaints
Charitey respects intellectual property rights and takes copyright infringement seriously. If you believe that content on the Platform infringes your copyright, you may submit a copyright complaint as described below.
9.4 Reporting Copyright Infringement
To file a copyright complaint, please send a written notice to copyright@charitey.org containing the following information:
A description of the copyrighted work you claim has been infringed.
A description of the infringing material and its location on the Platform (URL or platform-specific identifier).
Your full name, postal address, email address, and phone number.
A statement that you have a good faith belief that the disputed use is not authorised by the copyright owner, its agent, or the law.
A statement that the information in your notice is accurate and, under penalty of perjury, that you are the copyright owner or are authorised to act on the owner's behalf.
Your electronic or physical signature.
Charitey will review valid complaints promptly and will take appropriate action, including removal of infringing content, where warranted.
9.5 Counter Notice
If content you submitted has been removed as a result of a copyright complaint and you believe the removal was in error, you may submit a counter notice to copyright@charitey.org. The counter notice should include:
Identification of the removed content and its former location.
A statement under penalty of perjury that you believe the content was removed by mistake or misidentification.
Your contact details.
Your consent to the jurisdiction of the appropriate court.
Charitey will restore removed content at its discretion upon receipt of a valid counter notice, subject to applicable law.
9.6 Repeat Infringers
Charitey has a policy of terminating accounts of users who are found to be repeat infringers of intellectual property rights. We reserve the right to determine, at our sole discretion, whether a user is a repeat infringer.
